CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
Footnote 18.
— Not Available
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
Footnote 18.
— Not Available
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
Footnote 18.
— Not Available
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
Footnote 18.
— Not Available

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-12-04 11 3 9 165 1 165
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2025-04-17 99 13 91 912 1 912
Total weighted health score
351.8
lower is better

Deficiencies (120)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

2 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
108 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
5 G/H/I Actual harm
5 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-12-04 2026-01-13
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2025-12-04 2026-01-13
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2025-12-04 2026-01-13
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. K 2025-07-23 2025-08-28
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. C 2025-07-23 2025-08-28
0745 Provide medically-related social services to help each resident achieve the highest possible quality of life. D 2025-07-23 2025-08-28
0942 Ensure that staff members are educated on resident rights and facility responsibilities to properly care for its residents. D 2025-07-23 2025-08-28
0943 Give their staff education on dementia care, and what abuse, neglect, and exploitation are; and how to report abuse, neglect, and exploitation. D 2025-07-23 2025-08-28
0944 Conduct mandatory training, for all staff, on the facility’s Quality Assurance and Performance Improvement Program. D 2025-07-23 2025-08-28
0949 Provide behavior health training consistent with the requirements and as determined by a facility assessment. E 2025-07-23 2025-08-28
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2025-04-17 2025-05-23
0553 Allow resident to participate in the development and implementation of his or her person-centered plan of care. D 2025-04-17 2025-05-23
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2025-04-17 2025-05-23
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. J 2025-04-17 2025-05-23
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. G 2025-04-17 2025-05-23
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2025-04-17 2025-05-23
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2025-04-17 2025-05-23
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2025-04-17 2025-05-23
0926 Have policies on smoking. E 2025-04-17 2025-05-23
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. D 2025-04-17 2025-05-23
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. F 2025-04-17 2025-05-23
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2025-04-17 2025-05-23
0882 Designate a qualified infection preventionist to be responsible for the infection prevent and control program in the nursing home. F 2025-04-17 2025-05-23
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. D 2025-04-17 2025-05-23
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2025-01-15 2025-02-13
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2025-01-15 2025-02-13
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2025-01-15 2025-02-13
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. J 2025-01-15 2025-05-23
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-01-15 2025-02-13
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. D 2025-01-15 2025-02-13
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. F 2025-01-15 2025-02-13
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. J 2025-01-15 2025-02-13
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2025-01-15 2025-02-13
0837 Establish a governing body that is legally responsible for establishing and implementing policies for managing and operating the facility and appoints a properly licensed administrator responsible for managing the facility. F 2025-01-15 2025-02-13
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-01-15 2025-02-13
0941 Develop, implement, and/or maintain an effective training program that includes effective communications for direct care staff members. F 2025-01-15 2025-02-13
0942 Ensure that staff members are educated on resident rights and facility responsibilities to properly care for its residents. D 2025-01-15 2025-02-13
0943 Give their staff education on dementia care, and what abuse, neglect, and exploitation are; and how to report abuse, neglect, and exploitation. D 2025-01-15 2025-02-13
0944 Conduct mandatory training, for all staff, on the facility’s Quality Assurance and Performance Improvement Program. F 2025-01-15 2025-02-13
0945 Include as part of its infection prevention and control program, mandatory training that includes written standards, policies, and procedures for the program. D 2025-01-15 2025-02-13
0946 Provide training in compliance and ethics. F 2025-01-15 2025-02-13
0949 Provide behavior health training consistent with the requirements and as determined by a facility assessment. F 2025-01-15 2025-02-13
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2024-09-11 2024-10-18
0553 Allow resident to participate in the development and implementation of his or her person-centered plan of care. D 2024-09-11 2024-10-18
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2024-09-11 2024-10-18
0559 Honor the resident's right to share a room with spouse or roommate of choice and receive written notice before a change is made. D 2024-09-11 2024-10-18
0563 Honor the resident's right to receive visitors of his or her choosing, at the time of his or her choosing. D 2024-09-11 2024-10-18
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2024-09-11 2024-10-18
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2024-09-11 2024-10-18
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2024-09-11 2024-10-18
